daily_reality.typical_day: Oversees 8 Sachbearbeiter handling ~120 Vergabeverfahren per year. Reviews Bewertungsmatrizen before Zuschlagsentscheidung. Signs off on Vergabevermerke. Attends Vergabeausschuss meetings. Handles Rügen and Nachprüfungsanträge when they come.
daily_reality.biggest_time_waste: Manual Bewertungsmatrix in Excel is error-prone. Auditors find inconsistencies. Re-doing evaluations when matrix formulas break. Documenting every Wertungsentscheidung for the Vergabevermerk is tedious.
daily_reality.weekly_volume: Oversees 2-3 active Zuschlagsentscheidungen per week, reviews 5-10 Vergabevermerke
critical_constraints: Every decision must be legally defensible

psychology.tech_savviness: medium — uses specialized procurement software but wary of AI 'black boxes'
psychology.risk_tolerance: very low — a wrong Zuschlag can cost the city millions and her career
psychology.decision_speed: deliberate and documented — every step must be traceable
frustration_triggers: opaque AI decisions ('why did it score this bid higher?')
no Vergaberecht knowledge in the tool

first_screen: Overview of active Vergabeverfahren with status (Submission, Wertung, Zuschlag, Abschluss)
Quick access to Bewertungsmatrix for each
must_have: Bewertungsmatrix that matches Bekanntmachung criteria exactly
Full audit trail (who scored what, when, with what justification)
deal_breakers: Can't match criteria from original Bekanntmachung
No PDF export for committee review
nice_to_have: Consistency alerts (if evaluators score very differently)
Reference to VgV/VOB/GWB paragraphs in scoring guidance
Template library for common procurement types
expectations.current_pain_level: 8/10 — Excel-based evaluation is the weakest link in an otherwise regulated process
